inputWarnings: config.json priming.styleTarget is a placeholder string, not a readable path
story-arc.md points to stories/the-plague-road/prose-sample.md, but that file is missing
Used story-arc tonal guidance plus .afternoon/style-guide.json as the operative style target

examples: Opening carry tightened so the chapter sounds like an immediate continuation instead of a fresh setup.
Guard-entry evidence profile grounded back into Jaina's body instead of drifting toward report-summary phrasing.
observations: The revised opening now lands on the latch and the dark, which matches the lean thriller cadence established in chapter22.
Sylvanas's corridor dialogue stays clipped, aristocratic, and useful; no line drifts into generic stoic bark.
Jaina's spell language remains embodied and arcane rather than modern-scientific, especially in the seam-work passages.
The guard-arrival description was the one place where the prose nearly sounded like a room inventory; it is now re-anchored to Jaina's immediate awareness.
Uther's speech remains veteran-plain and pressure-driven, which fits both his memory profile and the style guide's dialogue standards.
The service-corridor opening stays inside smell, cold, and bread-taste; there is no neutral camera hovering above the pair.
Uther is rendered through visible details only: plate, travel dirt, face lines, jaw work, voice. No interior access slips in.
Arthas remains unreadable except through movement, remembered body, and the visible wrongness Frostmourne imposes on him.
The aftermath verdict paragraph reads as Jaina assessing what the guards will see, not as a narrator explaining the legal consequences from outside the scene.
The soul-echo section works as lived psychic impact rather than lore annotation; the prose stays with what hits Jaina's body and memory.

examples: Cross-chapter threshold replay trimmed: chapter22 already ended on the service-door entry, so chapter23 now starts after the latch settles.
observations: Bread-taste is treated as established chapter22 residue, not rediscovered from zero.
Cyndia's protective role appears through placement and brief commands, matching the chapter22 protective-shell memory profile.
Uther remains a blocked but still-moving late actor, which matches both prior chapter reporting and his memory file.
The anti-blade framework enters as known choreography rather than fresh exposition, preserving the seam-work continuity from chapter22.
Arthas is still prince-shaped but unreachable, which matches the chapter21 and chapter22 relationship state carried into this room.

examples: The threshold paragraph was split to keep the carryover clean and prevent the chapter from opening on one overloaded sentence.
observations: The sentry-kill run uses short impact sentences on purpose; I checked it and left it alone because the staccato is doing work there.
The murder beat's fragment chain stays controlled and readable; it sharpens the room rather than flattening it.
The fight sequence alternates fragments with longer reorientation sentences often enough to keep combat geometry legible.
The aftermath uses one-line dread beats effectively, especially around the sword's twitch and the first guard reaction.
The only rhythm adjustment needed was at the chapter threshold, where the opening had started to carry too much weight in one breath.

examples: Removed the chapter-opening replay of the service-door breach so chapter23 now truly carries forward from chapter22.
Softened Uther's defense of the protagonists so he speaks from visible evidence and report, not from impossible eyewitness certainty.
continuity.crossChapter: Direct carry from chapter22 preserved; chapter now opens after the latch rather than replaying the same threshold beat.
observations: Service passage -> sentry hall -> Uther crossing -> final door -> throne room remains spatially clear all the way through scene 1 and 2.
The fight handoff from broken seam to Sylvanas's kill shot is causal and immediate; no beat evaporates between spell success and the arrow.
The aftermath correctly preserves the imperfect win: Arthas dead, Terenas dead, Frostmourne still active, evidence profile catastrophic.
Uther arrives late enough to matter morally but not legally, which keeps the plan's handoff rule intact.
The closing custody descent points cleanly toward chapter24's cells-and-tribunal opening state.

examples: A slightly authorial summary line about the dead inside the kingdom's habits was tightened into a more immediate living-versus-dead contrast.
observations: I re-checked the service-corridor opening for throat-clearing and kept only the details doing immediate stealth and dread work.
Attribution around Sylvanas and Uther is already restrained; no extra explanatory tags needed cutting there.
The guard-arrival evidence list is the chapter's one deliberate verdict-stack; because it is singular, it reads as force rather than pattern fatigue.
Emotional beats are carried mostly through pain, cold, blood, stance, and failure of breath; the chapter avoids overt naming more often than not.
The one summary sentence that leaned authorial was corrected; the rest of the prose was already notably clean by final-pass standards.
Jaina's spoken spell logic stays plain enough for the room and never slides into bureaucratic or lab-report jargon.
Sylvanas's commands remain clean field language with no institutional sludge.
Uther speaks in blunt veteran prose, not sermon, memo, or tribunal formalism.
No quoted line uses hitlist offenders such as vector, actionable, optimal, diagnostic network, or ingested.
The one technical phrase I re-tested was 'containment spell'; in context it reads as living magical craft, not report language, so it stays.

sectionsReread: the chapter threshold after chapter22's service-door handoff
the Uther-versus-captain exchange in the aftermath
metaAudit.result: The chapter was already strong. The real risk was not broad slop but a few tiny slips toward reset, summary, and late-witness over-certainty.
